It’s dropping down from a $1,299 full price, which we last featured when it fell to the $899 low at the end of March in the brand’s expanded/extended spring sale lineup. It popped back up to this second-lowest rate during the previous Easter Sale, and is continuing the $400 markdown in this new event. If you want to save a bit more money while gaining much of the same backup power support, you can also find the Explorer 1500 v2 power station at $699 shipped during this sale. items.title: Hackers breach Rockstar ahead of GTA 6 launch – here’s the ransom note: ‘FINAL WARNING PAY OR LEAK’
items.summary:
Rockstar got hacked again, in case you missed it, and the culprits are demanding it pays up by tomorrow or they will leak GTA 6 all over the interwebs. Or something, or not at all, because Rockstar has now released a statement saying the hackers didn’t actually steal anything of value (by the sounds of it).

items.title: Baseus’ adaptive ANC headphones cost a fraction of the big brands at just $30 (Save 50%)
items.summary:
Baseus Bass BH1 Adaptive ANC Headphones

Buy for $30 (Reg. $60)

What we love

While I currently rock a pair of Bose QC Ultra as my go-to ANC headphones, every time I try a budget pair of headphones from some of the popular budget brands I come away impressed by how well they perform given the price point. For that reason, it’s hard to argue with the value proposition, and at $30, these are certainly worth a peek.

items.title: Introduce your kid to electric riding with $150 savings on NAVEE’s K100 e-scooter at a new $100 low
items.summary:
NAVEE K100 Electric Scooter for Kids Ages 6-10

Buy for $100 (Reg. $250)

What we love

If you’re looking for an affordable means to introduce your kids to the world of electric scooters, this NAVEE K100 model is as smart an option as it is safe – plus, it’s hitting a new all-time low price here, too! Rated for ages six to 10, it possesses three riding modes that can grow along with your kid up to a 10 MPH top speed. What’s nice is the app-based smart controls that let you maintain a watchful eye over its settings while letting your child explore riding independence.
